channel.title: Frontion News - Tier 1 Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources: Reuters, AP News, BBC, AFP, Al Jazeera, The Guardian, NYT, Washington Post, Financial Times, The Economist, Bloomberg, WSJ, CNN, CBS News, NBC News, CNBC, NPR

item.title: Iran considering US proposal to end war
item.description: Iran is weighing a US proposal for a 14-point memorandum of understanding to end the conflict. The White House believes it could be closing in on a deal with Tehran after days of escalation.

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut targeting Hezbollah commander
item.description: Israeli forces conducted the first strike on Beirut's southern suburbs since the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire took effect on April 17, targeting a senior Hezbollah figure.

item.title: US fires on Iranian oil tanker amid war pressure
item.description: US military forces fired on an Iranian oil tanker as the Trump administration continues to pressure Tehran for a deal to end the ongoing conflict.

item.title: Russia violates Ukraine ceasefire, attacks kindergarten
item.description: Russia ignored Ukraine's unilateral ceasefire and attacked a kindergarten. Ukraine's president says Russia has "spurned the ceasefire" as Kyiv considers further action.

item.title: CNN founder Ted Turner dies at 87
item.description: Media mogul Ted Turner, who revolutionized TV news by launching CNN in 1980, has died at age 87. He pioneered the modern 24-hour news culture.

item.title: Japan's Trump Supporters Lose Faith Amid Iran War Chaos
item.description: Japanese supporters of Trump express disillusionment as the Iran war creates economic uncertainty and disrupts global trade routes critical to Japan's economy.

item.title: India Sidelined by Iran War as Regional Diplomacy Unravels
item.description: India finds itself marginalized in Middle East diplomacy as the Iran conflict intensifies, with Pakistan taking a more prominent role in regional negotiations.

item.title: Taiwan's Silicon Shield Exposed by Hormuz Crisis
item.description: Taiwan's semiconductor industry faces supply chain vulnerabilities as the Strait of Hormuz disruption exposes the fragility of the island's economic defense strategy.

item.title: Russian Commander Claims Western Funding for Mali Militants
item.description: Russian military commanders allege Western nations are funding militant groups in Mali, escalating tensions over influence in Africa's Sahel region.

item.title: North Korea declares it is not bound by nuclear non-proliferation treaty
item.description: North Korea has officially declared it is not bound by any treaty on nuclear non-proliferation, according to state media KCNA, signaling continued nuclear development.

item.title: North Korea revises constitution to drop reunification references
item.description: North Korea has revised its constitution to remove references to reunification of the Korean peninsula, signaling a harder stance toward South Korea.

item.title: Rubio to meet Pope Leo as Trump continues attacks on pontiff
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is scheduled to meet Pope Leo XIV as President Trump escalates his criticism of the pontiff over Middle East policy.

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: Israel conducted its first strike on Beirut since the Hezbollah ceasefire agreement, targeting what it says is a militant leader in the southern suburbs.

item.title: Ted Turner, CNN Founder and Media Pioneer, Dies at 87
item.description: Ted Turner, the media mogul who founded CNN and revolutionized 24-hour news broadcasting, has died at the age of 87.

item.title: France Deploys Aircraft Carrier Toward Strait of Hormuz
item.description: French aircraft carrier group is moving into the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden as European powers increase naval presence near the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran tensions.

item.title: Japan Nikkei Hits Record High Above 62,000 on Middle East Peace Hopes
item.description: Japan's Nikkei 225 surged past 62,000 for the first time as investors bet on potential diplomatic resolution to the Iran conflict.

item.title: Venezuela Rejects ICJ Jurisdiction in Guyana Oil Territory Dispute
item.description: Venezuela told the International Court of Justice it has no jurisdiction over its territorial dispute with Guyana regarding the oil-rich Essequibo region.

item.title: ECB Rate Hike Chances Rise Amid Inflation Pressures
item.description: European Central Bank board member Cipollone says chances of an ECB rate hike have increased as inflation pressures persist across the eurozone.

item.title: US Counterterrorism Strategy Adds Offensive Cyber Operations
item.description: The US has updated its counterterrorism strategy to include offensive cyber operations, reflecting evolving threats in the digital domain.

item.title: Pentagon AI Adoption Gives Cyber Criminals Nation-State Powers
item.description: Pentagon leaders embrace agentic AI but experts warn it is providing cyber criminals with nation-state level capabilities.

item.title: Trump Counterterrorism Strategy Brands Europe Terrorism Incubator
item.description: New Trump administration counterterrorism strategy labels Europe as an incubator for terrorism, straining transatlantic relations.

item.title: Japan Nikkei Surges Past 61,000 on Iran Deal Optimism
item.description: Japanese stocks rallied to new highs on reports of potential deal to end the Iran conflict, with Nikkei breaking through 61,000 for the first time.

item.title: Russia resumes forex purchases as Iran war oil windfall boosts revenues
item.description: Russia has started buying foreign currency again as oil revenues surge from the Iran conflict, providing a financial windfall for Moscow.

item.title: Iran reviews US peace proposal as Trump signals war may be ending
item.description: Iran says it is reviewing a comprehensive peace proposal from the United States as diplomatic momentum builds toward ending the conflict.

item.title: Ukraine accuses Russia of violating ceasefire proposed by Kyiv
item.description: Ukraine says Russia violated the ceasefire initiated by Kyiv, with attacks continuing despite Ukraine's unilateral pause in hostilities.
